commands.review_summary:
The PR routes Bedrock Mantle and HuggingFace model catalog success responses through the shared bounded JSON response reader and updates Bedrock Mantle discovery tests to use real Response bodies.

PR surface: Source +8, Tests +12. Total +20 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main reads Mantle and HuggingFace successful catalog responses with unbounded response.json calls. I did not run a failing oversized-response repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining action is contributor proof plus maintainer acceptance of the shared provider-catalog cap, not an automated code repair.

target.title: fix(agents): bound model catalog JSON response reads for bedrock-mantle and huggingface
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Amazon Bedrock Mantle and HuggingFace model catalog discovery both read their HTTP success responses via an unbounded `await response.json()`. The Mantle endpoint is a user-configured AWS endpoint, and the HuggingFace endpoint is an external router; either could stream an arbitrarily large body into memory before parsing, potentially causing OOM on the discovery hot path.

The gap is the same as the one fixed for OpenRouter model catalog (#95418, #95420) and Ollama model discovery (#96027). This PR applies the identical fix to the two remaining bundled providers with the same issue.

Both reads are now routed through the shared `readProviderJsonResponse` helper (from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/provider-http`, which internally uses `readResponseWithLimit` at a 16 MiB cap). On overflow the stream is cancelled and a labelled error is thrown; the existing fail-soft handlers in each caller return empty/fallback model lists, so a capped endpoint degrades gracefully.

Existing tests were updated to use real `Response` objects with JSON bodies (replacing plain `{ ok: true, json: async () => ... }` mocks) so they exercise the same code path as production.

## Evidence

Behavior addressed: Bedrock Mantle and HuggingFace model-discovery JSON response reads were unbounded; a hostile or misconfigured endpoint could exhaust process memory before the catalog was parsed.

Real environment tested: macOS, Node 22, local source checkout.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/amazon-bedrock-mantle extensions/huggingface --reporter=verbose
```

Evidence after fix:
```
Test Files  5 passed (5)
Tests  48 passed (48)
```

Observed result after fix: All 48 existing tests pass including the 8 discovery tests that now use real `Response` bodies. The `discoverMantleModels` and `fetchHuggingfaceModels` callers now cancel the stream and propagate a labelled error if the response body exceeds 16 MiB, matching the pattern used by OpenRouter (#95420) and Ollama (#96027).

What was not tested: Live Mantle/HuggingFace API calls with an oversized response; overflow path relies on the shared `readProviderJsonResponse` / `readResponseWithLimit` unit tests and the pattern proven in the prior model-catalog fixes.

AI-assisted.
target.author: hugenshen
